volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Epidicus (Section 5.2.723c - Section 5.2.732)

sections.label: Section 5.2.724
sections.original: Numquam hercle hodie , nisi supplicium mihi das , me solvi sinam .
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I ’ ll never allow myself to be unbound this day , unless you give me satisfaction .

sections.label: Section 5.2.725
sections.original: Optumum atque aequissumum oras . soccos , tunicam , pallium
sections.translation: You ask what ’ s very fair and very just . Shoes , a tunic , and a cloak I ’ ll give you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.726d
sections.translation: But after that ? There ’ s need for a new-made freed-man to pick a bit [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.727b
sections.translation: It shall be given ; I ’ ll find you food .

sections.label: Section 5.2.728
sections.original: Numquam hercle hodie , nisi me orassis , solves .
sections.translation: On my word , never this day shall you release me , unless you beg my pardon .

sections.label: Section 5.2.728b
sections.translation: I do entreat you , Epidicus , to forgive me , if unawares in fault I ’ ve done anything wrong .

sections.label: Section 5.2.730
sections.original: at ob eam rem liber esto .
sections.translation: But by way of return for this matter , be a free man .

sections.label: Section 5.2.730b
sections.translation: Against my inclination do I grant you this pardon , but I ’ m compelled by necessity . Unbind them then , if you choose .

sections.label: Section 5.2.732
sections.original: Hic is homo est qui libertatem malitia invenit sua .
sections.translation: ( coming forward .) This is that man who by his roguery [*] has gained his freedom . Clap your hands , and fare you well ! Stretch your loins , and rise from your seats .
